basic_details.job_title: KEA AO AAO Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 945 Agriculture Officer, Assistant Agriculture Officer Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Karnataka Government, Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA), Bengaluru
basic_details.post_name: 1. Agriculture Officer (Krushi Adhikari) | 2. Assistant Agriculture Officer (Sahayaka Krushi Adhikari)
basic_details.department: Department of Agriculture, Government of Karnataka
basic_details.short_information: KEA is hiring Agriculture Officer and Assistant Agriculture Officer posts under both Inland (KK) and Rural Pay Commission (RPC) cadres through competitive examination, last date 20 June 2026.

important_dates.exam_date: Paper-1: 25.07.2026 (KK Cadre) / 26.07.2026 (RPC Cadre) - 10:30 AM to 12:30 PM | Paper-2: Same dates - 2:30 PM to 4:30 PM

application_fee.general_fee: Rs. 750/- (Rs. 500 Application Fee + Rs. 250 Processing Fee) - Non-Refundable
application_fee.sc_fee: Rs. 500/- (Rs. 250 Application Fee + Rs. 250 Processing Fee) - Non-Refundable
application_fee.details: Fee must be paid online only. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded. | Notes on Fee: | PwD candidates with disability of more than 40% and those possessing a certificate from the District Medical Authority are eligible for the reduced PwD fee. Candidates who applied online as per the earlier notification of 18.04.2026 and have already paid the fee for the same post need not pay again for this revised notification for the same post (new applicants applying for the first time must pay the full fee). SC candidates possessing RD numbers for SC-A, SC-B, and SC-C categories must mandatorily enter their RD number in the online application as per Government directive. In the Agriculture Department, candidates may apply for both Agriculture Officer (Rs. 85 level) and Assistant Agriculture Officer (Rs. 85 level) posts through a single application, paying the fee once. For each additional post under the same programme, Rs. 100/- per post must be paid as application fee. Candidates appearing for multiple posts in the same programme and same examination will have their marks transferred for special final rank-list preparation by the department.

age_limit.details: As per the Karnataka State Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules 1977, candidates must be a minimum of 18 years of age as on the last date for submission of applications. Per Government Order No. DPAR (Services-2) 262 Sehime 2025 dated 29.01.2026, a one-time relaxation of 5 years in the upper age limit is provided. After availing this relaxation, candidates must not exceed the maximum age limits shown below. | In addition to the above, the following specific age relaxations are available as per Karnataka Civil Services (General Recruitment) Rules 1977:

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the last date for submission of applications. Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. | 1. Educational Qualification: | Important Notes on Qualification: | The degree must be from a University/Institute recognized by ICAR (Indian Council of Agriculture Research) or AICTE. Degrees from institutions accredited by UGC / AICTE running regular and distance education courses, and distance education programmes producing technical-level graduates, will not be considered for recruitment. If two candidates possess the same qualification as mentioned above, they will be treated equally at the recruitment stage. Candidates cannot withdraw their candidature after participating in the selection process. Candidates who have passed a higher qualifying degree and are appearing in the examination for a lower qualifying degree post are not eligible to apply. All candidates must possess the degree / diploma by the last date of application. Warning: Candidates currently enrolled in a higher qualification must not submit applications, as KAP will not be responsible if they are later found ineligible at the stage of the prior application process.
qualification.education_qualification: The State Government retains the right to conduct a full medical examination by a medical board and remove candidates who are declared medically unfit; this rule cannot be waived under any provision

salary_details.pay_scale: Agriculture Officer: Rs. 69,250 – 1,34,200 (Revised State Pay Scale 2024) | Assistant Agriculture Officer: Rs. 65,950 – 1,24,900
salary_details.probation period: 2 years for candidates selected through direct recruitment
salary_details.national pension: Applicable as per Government Order No. FD(SPIL) 04 PIT 2005 dated 31.03.2006 - National Pension Scheme (NPS) benefits applicable

selection_process: Paper Subject / Content Maximum Marks Duration

Paper-1 General Paper: Current Affairs, General Science, Karnataka History & Geography, Indian History & Geography, Social Science, General Mental Ability, Indian Constitution, Public Administration & Rural Development, Science & Technology, International Relations and Economy, Environmental Science, Logical Reasoning, Day-to-day Consumer topics, and Practical Knowledge from previously passed examinations. 300 2 Hours
Paper-2 Specific Paper (syllabus to be announced separately) 300 2 Hours

Paper-1 General Paper: (Same syllabus as Rs. 85 level posts - Current Affairs, General Science, Karnataka History & Geography, Indian History & Geography, Social Science, General Mental Ability, Indian Constitution, Public Administration & Rural Development, Science & Technology, International Relations and Economy, Environmental Science, Logical Reasoning, Day-to-day Consumer topics, Practical Knowledge from previously passed examinations.) 300 2 Hours
how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online only through the Karnataka PSC website. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before filling the online application form, candidates must read the notification carefully. Incorrect or false information submitted in the application may lead to cancellation of candidature and legal action as per rules.
Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded at the time of Application:
Consolidated Marksheet / All subject-wise semester marksheets and Photo-certificate (PDF) of the highest qualifying degree obtained up to the last date of application
important_instructions: Candidates must carefully fill all information correctly while registering online. After submission, download a copy and preserve it until the recruitment process is complete. No petition / representation should be sent to the authority after submission. Changes or corrections to any information submitted through the online application will not be permitted under any circumstances. Candidates currently in service / who have previously served, if selected, must submit a No Objection Certificate / Permission Certificate / Service Certificate from the employing recruitment authority before the date of appointment. Service details must be entered in the application; false information will lead to disqualification. If the last date for fee payment falls on a public holiday, the next working day shall be treated as the last date for fee payment. The mobile number and email address entered in the application are final. Changes in mobile number / email cannot be entertained subsequently through any correspondence. Candidates whose results are pending for the qualifying degree at the time of applying and who have not yet received their results by the last date of application are not eligible to apply. Candidates will not be able to raise disputes regarding the educational qualification certificate / other reservation certificates. If the authority resolves such disputes, their selection will be treated as final. Candidates must read the detailed notification / instructions / procedures available on the website before applying online; information entered in the application is final and no additional documents can be submitted later. If the application of a candidate is rejected for incomplete submission or any benefit availed from it, such candidates are directly responsible for it. Candidates must not engage in any malpractice during the examination or at any stage; those found doing so will be disqualified from the selection and may face criminal proceedings and disciplinary action. Score lists obtained by candidates will be transferred to the concerned department / organization. After a written examination, all subsequent processes will be conducted as per rules of that department / organization. All recruitment-related information is available on the official account '@KEA_KARNATAKA' on the authority’s website.
